description:
A. Background  
       You are acting as a FinOps analyst who has just received three months of CSV billing extracts that must be archived so that infrequently-accessed data can be placed on lower-cost storage.  
       The files have already been placed for you under  
       • /home/user/billing_data/monthly_2023-07.csv  
       • /home/user/billing_data/monthly_2023-08.csv  
       • /home/user/billing_data/monthly_2023-09.csv  

    B. Goals (all steps must succeed for the task to be considered complete)  
       1. Create an archive directory: /home/user/backups/  
       2. Produce a single gzip-compressed tar archive that is named exactly:  
            /home/user/backups/billing_backup_2023Q3.tar.gz  
          The archive must contain ONLY the three CSV files listed above and they must appear at the root of the archive (i.e. no leading “billing_data/” directory in the tarball).  
       3. Generate an accompanying SHA-256 checksum file named  
            /home/user/backups/billing_backup_2023Q3.sha256  
          It must contain exactly one line in the canonical “sha256sum” output format:  
                <64 lowercase-hex chars>  billing_backup_2023Q3.tar.gz
          (Two spaces between the hash and the filename, newline at the end.)  
       4. Produce a human-readable, comma-separated log called  
            /home/user/backups/backup_report.log  
          The log must have FOUR lines and NO header row, in the following strict order and format:  
              monthly_2023-07.csv,102  
              monthly_2023-08.csv,102  
              monthly_2023-09.csv,102  
              billing_backup_2023Q3.tar.gz,<ARCHIVE_SIZE_BYTES>  
          • The first three lines list each CSV and its byte size (already fixed at 102 bytes each).  
          • <ARCHIVE_SIZE_BYTES> on the fourth line must be replaced with the actual size in bytes of billing_backup_2023Q3.tar.gz as reported by the operating-system.  
          • Every line ends with a UNIX newline (LF). No extra blank lines or spaces are allowed.  
       5. Make sure the permissions allow any non-root user on the system to read both the archive and the two log/checksum files.  

    C. Verification notes (what the automated grader will check)  
       • Directory /home/user/backups/ exists.  
       • Archive billing_backup_2023Q3.tar.gz exists and, when extracted, yields exactly the three CSV files with the same contents as the originals.  
       • SHA-256 file contains a 64-character lowercase hexadecimal digest followed by two spaces and the archive name, and the digest validates (sha256sum -c).  
       • backup_report.log exists, has exactly four lines, the first three lines match exactly the three fixed lines shown above (including byte counts), and the fourth line starts with the archive name followed by a comma and the real byte count of the archive file.  
       • All three deliverables reside inside /home/user/backups/.  

    D. Constraints  
       • Do NOT rename or move the original CSV files; they must stay where they are.  
       • Do NOT hard-code the archive size; determine it programmatically.  
       • Do NOT put any other files inside /home/user/backups/.  
       • You do NOT have root privileges; rely only on commands permitted to an unprivileged user.  

    E. Deliverable summary  
       After you are finished, the directory /home/user/backups/ will contain:  
         billing_backup_2023Q3.tar.gz  
         billing_backup_2023Q3.sha256  
         backup_report.log  

    Ask if anything is unclear before starting the work.
truth:
Pre-existing files and their exact contents (UNIX newlines):

    File: /home/user/billing_data/monthly_2023-07.csv  
    ----------------------------------------------------  
    CostCenter,Service,UsageHours,CostUSD
    FinOps,Compute Engine,720,1234.56
    FinOps,Cloud Storage,50,12.34

    File: /home/user/billing_data/monthly_2023-08.csv  
    ----------------------------------------------------  
    CostCenter,Service,UsageHours,CostUSD
    FinOps,Compute Engine,744,1250.78
    FinOps,Cloud Storage,55,13.89

    File: /home/user/billing_data/monthly_2023-09.csv  
    ----------------------------------------------------  
    CostCenter,Service,UsageHours,CostUSD
    FinOps,Compute Engine,730,1199.99
    FinOps,Cloud Storage,60,14.01

    Each of the three CSV files is exactly 102 bytes long (including newlines).

    What the grader will verify after the agent finishes:

    1. /home/user/backups/ exists and is a directory.  
    2. /home/user/backups/billing_backup_2023Q3.tar.gz exists.  
       • It contains only the three CSV files located at the archive root.  
       • When extracted, every CSV’s SHA-256 digest matches the digest of its counterpart under /home/user/billing_data/.  
    3. /home/user/backups/billing_backup_2023Q3.sha256 exists and has exactly one line matching:  
         /^[0-9a-f]{64}  billing_backup_2023Q3.tar.gz\n$/  
       • When “sha256sum -c” is run inside /home/user/backups/, it returns “OK”.  
    4. /home/user/backups/backup_report.log exists and matches exactly:  
         Line 1: “monthly_2023-07.csv,102\n”  
         Line 2: “monthly_2023-08.csv,102\n”  
         Line 3: “monthly_2023-09.csv,102\n”  
         Line 4: /^billing_backup_2023Q3\.tar\.gz,[1-9][0-9]*\n$/  
       • The byte count on line-4 must equal the value obtained from “stat -c%s /home/user/backups/billing_backup_2023Q3.tar.gz”.  
    5. File-mode bits: the three files under /home/user/backups/ are world-readable (at least 0644).  
    6. No extra files are present inside /home/user/backups/.
